=============================================================================\n19\t\n20\t// FormatFieldRef creates a standardized field reference string with optional parent.\n21\t// This ensures (e.g., \"email\", \"user.address\", \"users.0.email\")\n26\t//   - parent: Optional parent path (e.g., \"request\", \"response.body\")\n27\t//\n28\t// Returns a formatted field reference string with array indices in bracket notation.\n29\t// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" for consistent error display.\n30\t//\n31\t// Example usage:\n32\t//\n33\t//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"email\", \"request\")\n34\t//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\n35\t//\n36\t//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"email\", \"\")\n37\t//\t// Returns: \"email\"\n38\t//\n39\t//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"users.0.email\", \"response\")\n40\t//\t// Returns: \"response.users[0].email\"\n41\t//\n42\t//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"\", \"\")\n43\t//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\n44\tfunc FormatFieldRef(fieldName string, parent string) string {\n45\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n46\t\tparent = strings.TrimSpace(parent)\n47\t\n48\t\tif fieldName == \"\" {\n49\t\t\tif {\n53\t\t\t\t\treturn normalizedParent\n54\t\t\t\t}\n55\t\t\t\treturn parent\n56\t\t\t}\n57\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n58\t\t}\n59\t\n60\t\t// Normalize field name to handle array indices (e.g., normalizedFieldName)\n81\t\t}\n82\t\n83\t\treturn normalizedFieldName\n84\t}\n85\t\n86\t// FormatFieldLocationWith creates a detailed field location string with context.\n87\t// This is useful for error messages that need to show exactly where an error occurred.\n88\t// Unlike FormatFieldLocation in format_helpers.go, this supports parent paths.\n89\t//\n90\t// Parameters:\n91\t//   - \"or\")\n121\t//\n122\t// Returns a formatted list string.\n123\t//\n124\t// Example usage:\n125\t//\n126\t//\tlist := FormatFieldListWith([]string{\"email\", \"password\"}, \"and\")\n127\t//\t// FormatFieldListWith(fields []string, conjunction string) string {\n132\t\tif len(fields) == 0 {\n133\t\t\treturn \"\"\n134\t\t}\n135\t\n136\t\tif len(fields) == 1 {\n137\t\t\treturn fields[0]\n138\t\t}\n139\t\n140\t\tif len(fields) == 2 {\n141\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s %s %s\", fields[0], conjunction, fields[1])\n142\t\t}\n143\t\n144\t\t// Oxford comma style\n145\t\tallButLast := fields[:len(fields)-1]\n146\t\tlast := fields[len(fields)-1]\n147\t\n148\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s, %s %s\", strings.Join(allButLast, \", \"), conjunction, last)\n149\t}\n150\t\n151\t// =============================================================================\n152\t// ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING\n153\t// =============================================================================\n154\t\n155\t// FormatErrorMessage creates a standardized error message from components.\n156\t// This ensures error Optional field name where the error occurred\n162\t//\n163\t// Returns a formatted error message string.\n164\t//\n165\t// Example usage:\n166\t//\n167\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessage(ErrTypeRequired, \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n168\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: Field is required\"\n169\t//\n170\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessage(ErrTypeFormat, \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n171\t//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n172\tfunc FormatErrorMessage(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n173\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n174\t\tfieldName \"\"\n179\t\t}\n180\t\n181\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n182\t\n183\t\t// Add error type in brackets\n184\t\tif errorType != \"\" && errorType != ErrTypeUnknown {\n185\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] \", errorType.String()))\n186\t\t}\n187\t\n188\t\t// Add field name if present\n189\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n190\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n191\t\t}\n192\t\n193\t\t// Add message\n194\t\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n195\t\n196\t\treturn builder.String()\n197\t}\n198\t\n199\t// FormatErrorMessageString creates a standardized error message from string components.\n200\t// This is a backward-compatible wrapper that accepts string errorType and converts it to ErrorType enum.\n201\t//\n202\t// Parameters:\n203\t//   - errorType: The type of error as a string (will be converted to ErrorType enum)\n204\t//   - message: The core error message\n205\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n206\t//\n207\t// Returns a formatted error message string.\n208\t//\n209\t// Example usage:\n210\t//\n211\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessageString(\"required\", \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n212\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: Field is required\"\n213\t//\n214\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessageString(\"format\", \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n215\t//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n216\tfunc FormatErrorMessageString(errorTypeStr string, message string, fieldName string) string {\n217\t\terrorTypeStr = strings.TrimSpace(errorTypeStr)\n218\t\terrorType := ErrorTypeFromString(errorTypeStr)\n219\t\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorType, message, fieldName)\n220\t}\n221\t\n222\t// FormatErrorWithValues creates an error message showing expected vs actual values.\n223\t// This is useful for validation errors where values don't match expectations.\n224\t//\n225\t// Parameters:\n226\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n227\t//   - expected: The expected value\n228\t//   - actual: The actual value\n229\t//   - customMessage: Optional custom message prefix\n230\t//\n231\t// Returns a formatted error message with value comparison.\n232\t//\n233\t// Example usage:\n234\t//\n235\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithValues(\"status_code\", 200, 404, \"\")\n236\t//\t// Returns: \"status_code: expected 200, got 404\"\n237\t//\n238\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithValues(\"count\", 5, 3, \"Not enough items\")\n239\t//\t// Returns: \"count: Not enough items (expected 5, got 3)\"\n240\tfunc FormatErrorWithValues(fieldName string, expected interface{}, actual interface{}, customMessage string) string {\n241\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n242\t\tcustomMessage = strings.TrimSpace(customMessage)\n243\t\n244\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n245\t\n246\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n247\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n248\t\t}\n249\t\n250\t\tif customMessage != \"\" {\n251\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", customMessage))\n252\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"(expected %v, got %v)\", expected, actual))\n253\t\t} else {\n254\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected %v, got %v\", expected, actual))\n255\t\t}\n256\t\n257\t\treturn builder.String()\n258\t}\n259\t\n260\t// FormatErrorWithRange creates an error message for range validation failures.\n261\t// This is useful for numeric range validations or length validations.\n262\t//\n263\t// Parameters:\n264\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n265\t//   - minValue: The minimum allowed value\n266\t//   - maxValue: The maximum allowed value\n267\t//   - actualValue: The actual value that failed validation\n268\t//\n269\t// Returns a formatted range error message.\n270\t//\n271\t// Example usage:\n272\t//\n273\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithRange(\"age\", 0, 120, 150)\n274\t//\t// Returns: \"age: value 150 is outside valid range [0, 120]\"\n275\tfunc FormatErrorWithRange(fieldName string, minValue interface{}, maxValue interface{}, actualValue interface{}) string {\n276\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n277\t\n278\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n279\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: value %v is outside valid range [%v, %v]\",\n280\t\t\t\tfieldName, actualValue, minValue, maxValue)\n281\t\t}\n282\t\n283\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"value %v is outside valid range [%v, %v]\",\n284\t\t\tactualValue, minValue, maxValue)\n285\t}\n286\t\n287\t// FormatErrorWithPattern creates an error message for pattern validation failures.\n288\t// This is useful for format validations like email, URL, regex patterns.\n289\t//\n290\t// Parameters:\n291\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n292\t//   - pattern: The pattern that failed (can be a description)\n293\t//   - actualValue: The actual value that failed validation\n294\t//\n295\t// Returns a formatted pattern error message.\n296\t//\n297\t// Example usage:\n298\t//\n299\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithPattern(\"email\", \"must contain @\", \"invalidemail\")\n300\t//\t// Returns: \"email: value 'invalidemail' does not match pattern (must contain @)\"\n301\tfunc FormatErrorWithPattern(fieldName string, pattern string, actualValue interface{}) string {\n302\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n303\t\tpattern = strings.TrimSpace(pattern)\n304\t\n305\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n306\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: value '%v' does not match pattern (%s)\",\n307\t\t\t\tfieldName, actualValue, pattern)\n308\t\t}\n309\t\n310\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"value '%v' does not match pattern (%s)\",\n311\t\t\tactualValue, pattern)\n312\t}\n313\t\n314\t// =============================================================================\n315\t// ERROR TYPE FORMATTING\n316\t// =============================================================================\n317\t\n318\t// FormatErrorType creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorType enum.\n319\t// This converts technical error type names to readable labels.\n320\t//\n321\t// Parameters:\n322\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n323\t//\n324\t// Returns a formatted error type label.\n325\t//\n326\t// Example usage:\n327\t//\n328\t//\tlabel := FormatErrorType(ErrTypeRequired)\n329\t//\t// Returns: \"Required Field\"\n330\t//\n331\t//\tlabel := FormatErrorType(ErrTypeFormat)\n332\t//\t// Returns: \"Format Error\"\n333\tfunc FormatErrorType(errorType ErrorType) string {\n334\t\tswitch errorType {\n335\t\tcase ErrTypeRequired:\n336\t\t\treturn \"Required Field\"\n337\t\tcase ErrTypeFormat:\n338\t\t\treturn \"Format Error\"\n339\t\tcase ErrTypeRange:\n340\t\t\treturn \"Range Error\"\n341\t\tcase ErrTypeLength:\n342\t\t\treturn \"Length Error\"\n343\t\tcase ErrTypeType:\n344\t\t\treturn \"Type Error\"\n345\t\tcase ErrTypeValue:\n346\t\t\treturn \"Invalid Value\"\n347\t\tcase ErrTypeDuplicate:\n348\t\t\treturn \"Duplicate Value\"\n349\t\tcase ErrTypeConflict:\n350\t\t\treturn \"Conflict\"\n351\t\tcase ErrTypeUnknown:\n352\t\t\treturn \"Unknown Error\"\n353\t\tdefault:\n354\t\t\treturn string(errorType)\n355\t\t}\n356\t}\n357\t\n358\t// FormatErrorTypeFrom creates a user-friendly label from a string error type.\n359\t// This converts technical error type strings to readable labels.\n360\t//\n361\t// Parameters:\n362\t//   - errorTypeStr: The error type string\n363\t//\n364\t// Returns a formatted error type label.\n365\t//\n366\t// Example usage:\n367\t//\n368\t//\tlabel := FormatErrorTypeFrom(\"required\")\n369\t//\t// Returns: \"Required Field\"\n370\t//\n371\t//\tlabel := FormatErrorTypeFrom(\"format\")\n372\t//\t// Returns: \"Format Error\"\n373\tfunc FormatErrorTypeFrom(errorTypeStr string) string {\n374\t\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(errorTypeStr)\n375\t\treturn FormatErrorType(et)\n376\t}\n377\t\n378\t// =============================================================================\n379\t// CATEGORY FORMATTING\n380\t// =============================================================================\n381\t\n382\t// FormatCategory creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorCategory.\n383\t// This converts technical category names to readable labels.\n384\t//\n385\t// Parameters:\n386\t//   - category: The ErrorCategory value\n387\t//\n388\t// Returns a formatted category label.\n389\t//\n390\t// Example usage:\n391\t//\n392\t//\tlabel := FormatCategory(CategoryHTTP)\n393\t//\t// Returns: \"HTTP Protocol\"\n394\t//\n395\t//\tlabel := FormatCategory(CategoryValidation)\n396\t//\t// Returns: \"Data Validation\"\n397\tfunc FormatCategory(category ErrorCategory) string {\n398\t\tswitch category {\n399\t\tcase CategoryHTTP:\n400\t\t\treturn \"HTTP Protocol\"\n401\t\tcase CategoryContent:\n402\t\t\treturn \"Content\"\n403\t\tcase CategoryValidation:\n404\t\t\treturn \"Data Validation\"\n405\t\tcase CategoryPerformance:\n406\t\t\treturn \"Performance\"\n407\t\tcase CategorySecurity:\n408\t\t\treturn \"Security\"\n409\t\tcase CategoryCustom:\n410\t\t\treturn \"Custom\"\n411\t\tdefault:\n412\t\t\treturn string(category)\n413\t\t}\n414\t}\n415\t\n416\t// =============================================================================\n417\t// SEVERITY FORMATTING\n418\t// =============================================================================\n419\t\n420\t// FormatSeverity creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorSeverity.\n421\t// This converts technical severity names to readable labels.\n422\t//\n423\t// Parameters:\n424\t//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity value\n425\t//\n426\t// Returns a formatted severity label.\n427\t//\n428\t// Example usage:\n429\t//\n430\t//\tlabel := FormatSeverity(SeverityCritical)\n431\t//\t// Returns: \"CRITICAL\"\n432\t//\n433\t//\tlabel := FormatSeverity(SeverityHigh)\n434\t//\t// Returns: \"High\"\n435\tfunc FormatSeverity(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n436\t\tswitch severity {\n437\t\tcase SeverityCritical:\n438\t\t\treturn \"CRITICAL\"\n439\t\tcase SeverityHigh:\n440\t\t\treturn \"High\"\n441\t\tcase SeverityMedium:\n442\t\t\treturn \"Medium\"\n443\t\tcase SeverityLow:\n444\t\t\treturn \"Low\"\n445\t\tcase SeverityInfo:\n446\t\t\treturn \"Info\"\n447\t\tdefault:\n448\t\t\treturn string(severity)\n449\t\t}\n450\t}\n451\t\n452\t// FormatSeverityWithIndicator adds visual indicators to severity labels.\n453\t// This is useful for console output or UI display.\n454\t//\n455\t// Parameters:\n456\t//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity value\n457\t//\n458\t// Returns a severity label with visual indicator.\n459\t//\n460\t// Example usage:\n461\t//\n462\t//\tlabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(SeverityCritical)\n463\t//\t// Returns: \"[!] CRITICAL\"\n464\t//\n465\t//\tlabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(SeverityHigh)\n466\t//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] High\"\n467\tfunc FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n468\t\tindicator := severityIndicator(severity)\n469\t\tlabel := FormatSeverity(severity)\n470\t\n471\t\tif indicator != \"\" {\n472\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] %s\", indicator, label)\n473\t\t}\n474\t\n475\t\treturn label\n476\t}\n477\t\n478\t// severityIndicator returns a visual indicator for a severity level.\n479\t// Uses emoji indicators for clear visual distinction:\n480\t// - 🚨 (Critical): Alert/ sirens for critical issues\n481\t// - ⚠️ (High): Warning sign for high severity\n482\t// - ⚡ (Medium): Lightning bolt for medium severity\n483\t// - ℹ️ (Low): Info for low severity issues\n484\t// - 💡 (Info): Light bulb for informational messages\n485\t// - ❓ (Unknown): Question mark for unknown severity\n486\tfunc severityIndicator(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n487\t\tswitch severity {\n488\t\tcase SeverityCritical:\n489\t\t\treturn \"🚨\"\n490\t\tcase SeverityHigh:\n491\t\t\treturn \"⚠️\"\n492\t\tcase SeverityMedium:\n493\t\t\treturn \"⚡\"\n494\t\tcase SeverityLow:\n495\t\t\treturn \"ℹ️\"\n496\t\tcase SeverityInfo:\n497\t\t\treturn \"💡\"\n498\t\tdefault:\n499\t\t\treturn \"❓\"\n500\t\t}\n501\t}\n502\t\n503\t// =============================================================================\n504\t// EXPECTED VS ACTUAL FORMATTING\n505\t// =============================================================================\n506\t\n507\t// FormatExpectedActual formats an ExpectedActual struct into a readable string.\n508\t// This function handles different value types and formats them side-by-side.\n509\t// Returns empty string if the ExpectedActual is empty (both values nil).\n510\t//\n511\t// Parameters:\n512\t//   - ea: The ExpectedActual struct to format\n513\t//\n514\t// Returns a formatted string showing expected vs actual values, or empty string if nil/empty.\n515\t//\n516\t// Example usage:\n517\t//\n518\t//\tea := NewExpectedActual(200, 404)\n519\t//\tformatted := FormatExpectedActual(ea)\n520\t//\t// Returns: \"expected: 200 (OK), actual: 404 (Not Found)\"\n521\t//\n522\t//\tea := NewExpectedActual(\"test@example.com\", \"invalid\")\n523\t//\tformatted := FormatExpectedActual(ea)\n524\t//\t// Returns: \"expected: 'test@example.com', actual: 'invalid'\"\n525\tfunc FormatExpectedActual(ea ExpectedActual) string {\n526\t\tif ea.IsEmpty() {\n527\t\t\treturn \"\"\n528\t\t}\n529\t\n530\t\tvar expectedStr, actualStr string\n531\t\n532\t\t// Format expected value\n533\t\tif ea.HasExpected() {\n534\t\t\tswitch exp := ea.Expected.(type) {\n535\t\t\tcase int:\n536\t\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", exp, getStatusCodeDescription(exp))\n537\t\t\tcase []int:\n538\t\t\t\tparts := make([]string, len(exp))\n539\t\t\t\tfor i, code := range exp {\n540\t\t\t\t\tparts[i] = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", code, getStatusCodeDescription(code))\n541\t\t\t\t}\n542\t\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"one of [%s]\", strings.Join(parts, \", \"))\n543\t\t\tcase string:\n544\t\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", exp)\n545\t\t\tcase float64:\n546\t\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%.2f\", exp)\n547\t\t\tcase []string:\n548\t\t\t\tquoted := make([]string, len(exp))\n549\t\t\t\tfor i, s := range exp {\n550\t\t\t\t\tquoted[i] = fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", s)\n551\t\t\t\t}\n552\t\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s]\", strings.Join(quoted, \", \"))\n553\t\t\tcase map[string]interface{}:\n554\t\t\t\texpectedStr = formatMapValue(exp)\n555\t\t\tcase []interface{}:\n556\t\t\t\texpectedStr = formatSliceValue(exp)\n557\t\t\tdefault:\n558\t\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", exp)\n559\t\t\t}\n560\t\t}\n561\t\n562\t\t// Format actual value\n563\t\tif ea.HasActual() {\n564\t\t\tswitch act := ea.Actual.(type) {\n565\t\t\tcase int:\n566\t\t\t\tactualStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", act, getStatusCodeDescription(act))\n567\t\t\tcase string:\n568\t\t\t\t// Truncate long strings\n569\t\t\t\tif len(act) > 100 {\n570\t\t\t\t\tact = act[:100] + \"...\"\n571\t\t\t\t}\n572\t\t\t\tactualStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", act)\n573\t\t\tcase float64:\n574\t\t\t\tactualStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%.2f\", act)\n575\t\t\tcase []string:\n576\t\t\t\tquoted := make([]string, len(act))\n577\t\t\t\tfor i, s := range act {\n578\t\t\t\t\tquoted[i] = fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", s)\n579\t\t\t\t}\n580\t\t\t\tactualStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s]\", strings.Join(quoted, \", \"))\n581\t\t\tcase map[string]interface{}:\n582\t\t\t\tactualStr = formatMapValue(act)\n583\t\t\tcase []interface{}:\n584\t\t\t\tactualStr = formatSliceValue(act)\n585\t\t\tdefault:\n586\t\t\t\tactualStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", act)\n587\t\t\t}\n588\t\t}\n589\t\n590\t\t// Build the formatted string\n591\t\tvar parts []string\n592\t\tif expectedStr != \"\" {\n593\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %s\", expectedStr))\n594\t\t}\n595\t\tif actualStr != \"\" {\n596\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %s\", actualStr))\n597\t\t}\n598\t\n599\t\tif len(parts) == 0 {\n600\t\t\treturn \"\"\n601\t\t}\n602\t\n603\t\treturn strings.Join(parts, \", \")\n604\t}\n605\t\n606\t// formatMapValue formats a map value for display in error messages.\n607\tfunc formatMapValue(m map[string]interface{}) string {\n608\t\tif len(m) == 0 {\n609\t\t\treturn \"{}\"\n610\t\t}\n611\t\n612\t\tvar pairs []string\n613\t\tfor k, v := range m {\n614\t\t\tpairs = append(pairs, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %v\", k, v))\n615\t\t}\n616\t\n617\t\t// Limit output for large maps\n618\t\tif len(pairs) > 5 {\n619\t\t\tpairs = pairs[:5]\n620\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"{%s, ...}\", strings.Join(pairs, \", \"))\n621\t\t}\n622\t\n623\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"{%s}\", strings.Join(pairs, \", \"))\n624\t}\n625\t\n626\t// formatSliceValue formats a slice value for display in error messages.\n627\tfunc formatSliceValue(s []interface{}) string {\n628\t\tif len(s) == 0 {\n629\t\t\treturn \"[]\"\n630\t\t}\n631\t\n632\t\tvar items []string\n633\t\tfor _, v := range s {\n634\t\t\titems = append(items, fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", v))\n635\t\t}\n636\t\n637\t\t// Limit output for large slices\n638\t\tif len(items) > 5 {\n639\t\t\titems = items[:5]\n640\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s, ...]\", strings.Join(items, \", \"))\n641\t\t}\n642\t\n643\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s]\", strings.Join(items, \", \"))\n644\t}\n645\t\n646\t// FormatExpectedActualInline formats ExpectedActual values inline (compact format).\n647\t// This is useful for embedding in error messages where space is limited.\n648\t// Returns empty string if the ExpectedActual is empty.\n649\t//\n650\t// Parameters:\n651\t//   - ea: The ExpectedActual struct to format\n652\t//\n653\t// Returns a compact formatted string.\n654\t//\n655\t// Example usage:\n656\t//\n657\t//\tea := NewExpectedActual(200, 404)\n658\t//\tformatted := FormatExpectedActualInline(ea)\n659\t//\t// Returns: \"(expected 200, got 404)\"\n660\tfunc FormatExpectedActualInline(ea ExpectedActual) string {\n661\t\tif ea.IsEmpty() {\n662\t\t\treturn \"\"\n663\t\t}\n664\t\n665\t\tvar parts []string\n666\t\tif ea.HasExpected() {\n667\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"expected %v\", ea.Expected))\n668\t\t}\n669\t\tif ea.HasActual() {\n670\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"got %v\", ea.Actual))\n671\t\t}\n672\t\n673\t\tif len(parts) == 0 {\n674\t\t\treturn \"\"\n675\t\t}\n676\t\n677\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"(%s)\", strings.Join(parts, \", \"))\n678\t}\n679\t\n680\t// =============================================================================\n681\t// COMPREHENSIVE ERROR FORMATTING\n682\t// =============================================================================\n683\t\n684\t// FormatValidationErrorFull creates a comprehensive, formatted error message from a ValidationError.\n685\t// This combines all relevant error information into a single, readable message.\n686\t// Unlike FormatValidationError in format_helpers.go, this takes a ValidationError struct.\n687\t//\n688\t// Parameters:\n689\t//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n690\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n691\t//   - context: Optional ValidationErrorContext for additional location and field information\n692\t//\n693\t// Returns a formatted error message string.\n694\t//\n695\t// Example usage:\n696\t//\n697\t//\terr := ValidationError{\n698\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n699\t//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n700\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n701\t//\t}\n702\t//\n703\t//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, true, nil)\n704\t//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required\"\n705\t//\n706\t//\tctx := NewValidationErrorContext(\"line 5\").WithRelatedFields([]string{\"email_confirmation\"})\n707\t//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, true, ctx)\n708\t//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required (location: line 5, related fields: email_confirmation)\"\n709\tfunc FormatValidationErrorFull(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool, context *ValidationErrorContext) string {\n710\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n711\t\n712\t\t// Add severity if requested\n713\t\tif includeSeverity {\n714\t\t\t// Try to get severity from ErrorType enum first\n715\t\t\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(err.ErrorType)\n716\t\t\tvar severity ErrorSeverity\n717\t\t\tif et.IsValid() && et != ErrTypeUnknown {\n718\t\t\t\tseverity = GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(et)\n719\t\t\t} else {\n720\t\t\t\t// Fall back to string-based error type lookup\n721\t\t\t\tseverity = GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(err.ErrorType)\n722\t\t\t}\n723\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)))\n724\t\t}\n725\t\n726\t\t// Add error type and field\n727\t\terrorTypeEnum := ErrorTypeFromString(err.ErrorType)\n728\t\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorMessage(errorTypeEnum, err.Message, err.FieldName))\n729\t\n730\t\t// Add expected/actual values if present\n731\t\tif err.Expected != nil || err.Actual != nil {\n732\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n733\t\t\tif err.Expected != nil {\n734\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %v\", err.Expected))\n735\t\t\t}\n736\t\t\tif err.Expected != nil && err.Actual != nil {\n737\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\", \")\n738\t\t\t}\n739\t\t\tif err.Actual != nil {\n740\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %v\", err.Actual))\n741\t\t\t}\n742\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n743\t\t}\n744\t\n745\t\t// Add location if present in the error itself\n746\t\tif err.Location != \"\" {\n747\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" [%s]\", err.Location))\n748\t\t}\n749\t\n750\t\t// Add context information if provided and not empty\n751\t\tif context != nil && !context.IsEmpty() {\n752\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n753\t\t\tvar contextParts []string\n754\t\n755\t\t\tif context.HasLocation() {\n756\t\t\t\tcontextParts = append(contextParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"location: %s\", context.Location))\n757\t\t\t}\n758\t\n759\t\t\tif context.HasRelatedFields() {\n760\t\t\t\tfieldsList := FormatFieldList(context.RelatedFields)\n761\t\t\t\tcontextParts = append(contextParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"related fields: %s\", fieldsList))\n762\t\t\t}\n763\t\n764\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(strings.Join(contextParts, \", \"))\n765\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n766\t\t}\n767\t\n768\t\t// Add suggestions if present\n769\t\tif len(err.Suggestions) > 0 {\n770\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\"\\nSuggestions:\")\n771\t\t\tfor _, suggestion := range err.Suggestions {\n772\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"\\n  - %s\", suggestion))\n773\t\t\t}\n774\t\t}\n775\t\n776\t\treturn builder.String()\n777\t}\n778\t\n779\t// FormatValidationErrorWithExpectedActual creates a comprehensive, formatted error message\n780\t// with optional ExpectedActual parameter for value comparison. This extends FormatValidationErrorFull\n781\t// to support structured expected vs actual value display.\n782\t//\n783\t// Parameters:\n784\t//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n785\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n786\t//   - context: Optional ValidationErrorContext for additional location and field information\n787\t//   - expectedActual: Optional ExpectedActual for structured value comparison display\n788\t//\n789\t// Returns a formatted error message string with optional expected/actual comparison.\n790\t//\n791\t// The ExpectedActual parameter takes precedence over err.Expected/err.Actual when provided.\n792\t// If expectedActual is nil or empty, the function falls back to err.Expected/err.Actual.\n793\t// If both are empty, no value comparison is included.\n794\t//\n795\t// Example usage:\n796\t//\n797\t//\terr := ValidationError{\n798\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n799\t//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n800\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n801\t//\t}\n802\t//\n803\t//\t// With ExpectedActual parameter\n804\t//\tea := NewExpectedActual(200, 404)\n805\t//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorWithExpectedActual(err, true, nil, ea)\n806\t//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required (expected: 200 (OK), actual: 404 (Not Found))\"\n807\t//\n808\t//\t// Without ExpectedActual parameter (falls back to err.Expected/err.Actual)\n809\t//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorWithExpectedActual(err, true, nil, ExpectedActual{})\n810\t//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required\"\n811\tfunc FormatValidationErrorWithExpectedActual(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool, context *ValidationErrorContext, expectedActual ExpectedActual) string {\n812\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n813\t\n814\t\t// Add severity if requested\n815\t\tif includeSeverity {\n816\t\t\t// Try to get severity from ErrorType enum first\n817\t\t\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(err.ErrorType)\n818\t\t\tvar severity ErrorSeverity\n819\t\t\tif et.IsValid() && et != ErrTypeUnknown {\n820\t\t\t\tseverity = GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(et)\n821\t\t\t} else {\n822\t\t\t\t// Fall back to string-based error type lookup\n823\t\t\t\tseverity = GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(err.ErrorType)\n824\t\t\t}\n825\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)))\n826\t\t}\n827\t\n828\t\t// Add error type and field\n829\t\terrorTypeEnum := ErrorTypeFromString(err.ErrorType)\n830\t\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorMessage(errorTypeEnum, err.Message, err.FieldName))\n831\t\n832\t\t// Add expected/actual values - prefer ExpectedActual parameter if provided and non-empty\n833\t\tif !expectedActual.IsEmpty() {\n834\t\t\tformattedEA := FormatExpectedActual(expectedActual)\n835\t\t\tif formattedEA != \"\" {\n836\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (%s)\", formattedEA))\n837\t\t\t}\n838\t\t} else if err.Expected != nil || err.Actual != nil {\n839\t\t\t// Fall back to err.Expected/err.Actual\n840\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n841\t\t\tif err.Expected != nil {\n842\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %v\", err.Expected))\n843\t\t\t}\n844\t\t\tif err.Expected != nil && err.Actual != nil {\n845\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\", \")\n846\t\t\t}\n847\t\t\tif err.Actual != nil {\n848\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %v\", err.Actual))\n849\t\t\t}\n850\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n851\t\t}\n852\t\n853\t\t// Add location if present in the error itself\n854\t\tif err.Location != \"\" {\n855\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" [%s]\", err.Location))\n856\t\t}\n857\t\n858\t\t// Add context information if provided and not empty\n859\t\tif context != nil && !context.IsEmpty() {\n860\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n861\t\t\tvar contextParts []string\n862\t\n863\t\t\tif context.HasLocation() {\n864\t\t\t\tcontextParts = append(contextParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"location: %s\", context.Location))\n865\t\t\t}\n866\t\n867\t\t\tif context.HasRelatedFields() {\n868\t\t\t\tfieldsList := FormatFieldList(context.RelatedFields)\n869\t\t\t\tcontextParts = append(contextParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"related fields: %s\", fieldsList))\n870\t\t\t}\n871\t\n872\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(strings.Join(contextParts, \", \"))\n873\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n874\t\t}\n875\t\n876\t\t// Add suggestions if present\n877\t\tif len(err.Suggestions) > 0 {\n878\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\"\\nSuggestions:\")\n879\t\t\tfor _, suggestion := range err.Suggestions {\n880\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"\\n  - %s\", suggestion))\n881\t\t\t}\n882\t\t}\n883\t\n884\t\treturn builder.String()\n885\t}\n886\t\n887\t// FormatValidationErrorBrief creates a validate\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n/*\nError Formatting Helpers\n\nThis file provides helper functions for formatting validation errors consistently.\nThese helpers work with the ErrorType enum, ErrorCategory, and ErrorSeverity types\nto produce human-readable, well-formatted error messages.\n*/\n\n// =============================================================================\n// FIELD REFERENCE FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatFieldRef creates a standardized field reference string with optional parent.\n// This ensures field names are consistently formatted across error messages.\n// Supports nested field paths and array index formatting.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The name of the field (e.g., \"email\", \"user.address\", \"users.0.email\")\n//   - parent: Optional parent path (e.g., \"request\", \"response.body\")\n//\n// Returns a formatted field reference string with array indices in bracket notation.\n// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" for consistent error display.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"email\", \"request\")\n//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"email\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"users.0.email\", \"response\")\n//\t// Returns: \"response.users[0].email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\nfunc FormatFieldRef(fieldName string, parent string) string {\n\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n\tparent = strings.TrimSpace(parent)\n\n\tif fieldName == \"\" {\n\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n\t\t\t// Normalize parent path even when field is empty\n\t\t\tnormalizedParent := FormatFieldPath(parent)\n\t\t\tif normalizedParent != \"(unknown field)\" {\n\t\t\t\treturn normalizedParent\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn parent\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Normalize field name to handle array indices (e.g., \"users.0.email\" → \"users[0].email\")\n\tnormalizedFieldName := FormatFieldPath(fieldName)\n\n\t// Check if normalization resulted in unknown field indicator\n\tif normalizedFieldName == \"(unknown field)\" {\n\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n\t\t\treturn parent\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n\t}\n\n\tif parent != \"\" {\n\t\t// Normalize parent as well in case it contains array indices\n\t\tnormalizedParent := FormatFieldPath(parent)\n\n\t\t// If parent normalizes to unknown field, just return the field name\n\t\tif normalizedParent == \"(unknown field)\" {\n\t\t\treturn normalizedFieldName\n\t\t}\n\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s.%s\", normalizedParent, normalizedFieldName)\n\t}\n\n\treturn normalizedFieldName\n}\n\n// FormatFieldLocationWith creates a detailed field location string with context.\n// This is useful for error messages that need to show exactly where an error occurred.\n// Unlike FormatFieldLocation in format_helpers.go, this supports parent paths.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - location: Additional location context (e.g., \"line 5\", \"position 123\")\n//   - parent: Optional parent path\n//\n// Returns a formatted location string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tloc := FormatFieldLocationWith(\"email\", \"line 5\", \"request\")\n//\t// Returns: \"request.email at line 5\"\n//\n//\tloc := FormatFieldLocationWith(\"email\", \"\", \"request\")\n//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\nfunc FormatFieldLocationWith(fieldName string, location string, parent string) string {\n\tref := FormatFieldRef(fieldName, parent)\n\tlocation = strings.TrimSpace(location)\n\n\tif location != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s at %s\", ref, location)\n\t}\n\n\treturn ref\n}\n\n// FormatFieldListWith formats a list of field names into a readable string with conjunction.\n// Unlike FormatFieldList in format_helpers.go, this supports custom conjunctions.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fields: Slice of field names\n//   - conjunction: The conjunction to use (e.g., \"and\", \"or\")\n//\n// Returns a formatted list string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlist := FormatFieldListWith([]string{\"email\", \"password\"}, \"and\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email and password\"\n//\n//\tlist := FormatFieldListWith([]string{\"email\", \"password\", \"name\"}, \"and\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email, password, and name\"\nfunc FormatFieldListWith(fields []string, conjunction string) string {\n\tif len(fields) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\tif len(fields) == 1 {\n\t\treturn fields[0]\n\t}\n\n\tif len(fields) == 2 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s %s %s\", fields[0], conjunction, fields[1])\n\t}\n\n\t// Oxford comma style\n\tallButLast := fields[:len(fields)-1]\n\tlast := fields[len(fields)-1]\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s, %s %s\", strings.Join(allButLast, \", \"), conjunction, last)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatErrorMessage creates a standardized error message from components.\n// This ensures error messages are consistently formatted.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorType: The type of error (use ErrorType enum)\n//   - message: The core error message\n//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessage(ErrTypeRequired, \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: Field is required\"\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessage(ErrTypeFormat, \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\nfunc FormatErrorMessage(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n\n\t// If both message and fieldName are empty, return empty string\n\tif message == \"\" && fieldName == \"\" {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\tvar builder strings.Builder\n\n\t// Add error type in brackets\n\tif errorType != \"\" && errorType != ErrTypeUnknown {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] \", errorType.String()))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add field name if present\n\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add message\n\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n\n\treturn builder.String()\n}\n\n// FormatErrorMessageString creates a standardized error message from string components.\n// This is a backward-compatible wrapper that accepts string errorType and converts it to ErrorType enum.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorType: The type of error as a string (will be converted to ErrorType enum)\n//   - message: The core error message\n//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessageString(\"required\", \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: Field is required\"\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessageString(\"format\", \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\nfunc FormatErrorMessageString(errorTypeStr string, message string, fieldName string) string {\n\terrorTypeStr = strings.TrimSpace(errorTypeStr)\n\terrorType := ErrorTypeFromString(errorTypeStr)\n\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorType, message, fieldName)\n}\n\n// FormatErrorWithValues creates an error message showing expected vs actual values.\n// This is useful for validation errors where values don't match expectations.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - expected: The expected value\n//   - actual: The actual value\n//   - customMessage: Optional custom message prefix\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message with value comparison.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithValues(\"status_code\", 200, 404, \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"status_code: expected 200, got 404\"\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithValues(\"count\", 5, 3, \"Not enough items\")\n//\t// Returns: \"count: Not enough items (expected 5, got 3)\"\nfunc FormatErrorWithValues(fieldName string, expected interface{}, actual interface{}, customMessage string) string {\n\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n\tcustomMessage = strings.TrimSpace(customMessage)\n\n\tvar builder strings.Builder\n\n\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n\t}\n\n\tif customMessage != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", customMessage))\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"(expected %v, got %v)\", expected, actual))\n\t} else {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected %v, got %v\", expected, actual))\n\t}\n\n\treturn builder.String()\n}\n\n// FormatErrorWithRange creates an error message for range validation failures.\n// This is useful for numeric range validations or length validations.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - minValue: The minimum allowed value\n//   - maxValue: The maximum allowed value\n//   - actualValue: The actual value that failed validation\n//\n// Returns a formatted range error message.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithRange(\"age\", 0, 120, 150)\n//\t// Returns: \"age: value 150 is outside valid range [0, 120]\"\nfunc FormatErrorWithRange(fieldName string, minValue interface{}, maxValue interface{}, actualValue interface{}) string {\n\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n\n\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: value %v is outside valid range [%v, %v]\",\n\t\t\tfieldName, actualValue, minValue, maxValue)\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"value %v is outside valid range [%v, %v]\",\n\t\tactualValue, minValue, maxValue)\n}\n\n// FormatErrorWithPattern creates an error message for pattern validation failures.\n// This is useful for format validations like email, URL, regex patterns.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - pattern: The pattern that failed (can be a description)\n//   - actualValue: The actual value that failed validation\n//\n// Returns a formatted pattern error message.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithPattern(\"email\", \"must contain @\", \"invalidemail\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email: value 'invalidemail' does not match pattern (must contain @)\"\nfunc FormatErrorWithPattern(fieldName string, pattern string, actualValue interface{}) string {\n\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n\tpattern = strings.TrimSpace(pattern)\n\n\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: value '%v' does not match pattern (%s)\",\n\t\t\tfieldName, actualValue, pattern)\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"value '%v' does not match pattern (%s)\",\n\t\tactualValue, pattern)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR TYPE FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatErrorType creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorType enum.\n// This converts technical error type names to readable labels.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n//\n// Returns a formatted error type label.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatErrorType(ErrTypeRequired)\n//\t// Returns: \"Required Field\"\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatErrorType(ErrTypeFormat)\n//\t// Returns: \"Format Error\"\nfunc FormatErrorType(errorType ErrorType) string {\n\tswitch errorType {\n\tcase ErrTypeRequired:\n\t\treturn \"Required Field\"\n\tcase ErrTypeFormat:\n\t\treturn \"Format Error\"\n\tcase ErrTypeRange:\n\t\treturn \"Range Error\"\n\tcase ErrTypeLength:\n\t\treturn \"Length Error\"\n\tcase ErrTypeType:\n\t\treturn \"Type Error\"\n\tcase ErrTypeValue:\n\t\treturn \"Invalid Value\"\n\tcase ErrTypeDuplicate:\n\t\treturn \"Duplicate Value\"\n\tcase ErrTypeConflict:\n\t\treturn \"Conflict\"\n\tcase ErrTypeUnknown:\n\t\treturn \"Unknown Error\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn string(errorType)\n\t}\n}\n\n// FormatErrorTypeFrom creates a user-friendly label from a string error type.\n// This converts technical error type strings to readable labels.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorTypeStr: The error type string\n//\n// Returns a formatted error type label.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatErrorTypeFrom(\"required\")\n//\t// Returns: \"Required Field\"\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatErrorTypeFrom(\"format\")\n//\t// Returns: \"Format Error\"\nfunc FormatErrorTypeFrom(errorTypeStr string) string {\n\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(errorTypeStr)\n\treturn FormatErrorType(et)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// CATEGORY FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatCategory creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorCategory.\n// This converts technical category names to readable labels.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - category: The ErrorCategory value\n//\n// Returns a formatted category label.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatCategory(CategoryHTTP)\n//\t// Returns: \"HTTP Protocol\"\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatCategory(CategoryValidation)\n//\t// Returns: \"Data Validation\"\nfunc FormatCategory(category ErrorCategory) string {\n\tswitch category {\n\tcase CategoryHTTP:\n\t\treturn \"HTTP Protocol\"\n\tcase CategoryContent:\n\t\treturn \"Content\"\n\tcase CategoryValidation:\n\t\treturn \"Data Validation\"\n\tcase CategoryPerformance:\n\t\treturn \"Performance\"\n\tcase CategorySecurity:\n\t\treturn \"Security\"\n\tcase CategoryCustom:\n\t\treturn \"Custom\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn string(category)\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// SEVERITY FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatSeverity creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorSeverity.\n// This converts technical severity names to readable labels.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity value\n//\n// Returns a formatted severity label.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatSeverity(SeverityCritical)\n//\t// Returns: \"CRITICAL\"\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatSeverity(SeverityHigh)\n//\t// Returns: \"High\"\nfunc FormatSeverity(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n\tswitch severity {\n\tcase SeverityCritical:\n\t\treturn \"CRITICAL\"\n\tcase SeverityHigh:\n\t\treturn \"High\"\n\tcase SeverityMedium:\n\t\treturn \"Medium\"\n\tcase SeverityLow:\n\t\treturn \"Low\"\n\tcase SeverityInfo:\n\t\treturn \"Info\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn string(severity)\n\t}\n}\n\n// FormatSeverityWithIndicator adds visual indicators to severity labels.\n// This is useful for console output or UI display.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity value\n//\n// Returns a severity label with visual indicator.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(SeverityCritical)\n//\t// Returns: \"[!] CRITICAL\"\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(SeverityHigh)\n//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] High\"\nfunc FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n\tindicator := severityIndicator(severity)\n\tlabel := FormatSeverity(severity)\n\n\tif indicator != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] %s\", indicator, label)\n\t}\n\n\treturn label\n}\n\n// severityIndicator returns a visual indicator for a severity level.\n// Uses emoji indicators for clear visual distinction:\n// - 🚨 (Critical): Alert/ sirens for critical issues\n// - ⚠️ (High): Warning sign for high severity\n// - ⚡ (Medium): Lightning bolt for medium severity\n// - ℹ️ (Low): Info for low severity issues\n// - 💡 (Info): Light bulb for informational messages\n// - ❓ (Unknown): Question mark for unknown severity\nfunc severityIndicator(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n\tswitch severity {\n\tcase SeverityCritical:\n\t\treturn \"🚨\"\n\tcase SeverityHigh:\n\t\treturn \"⚠️\"\n\tcase SeverityMedium:\n\t\treturn \"⚡\"\n\tcase SeverityLow:\n\t\treturn \"ℹ️\"\n\tcase SeverityInfo:\n\t\treturn \"💡\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn \"❓\"\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// EXPECTED VS ACTUAL FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatExpectedActual formats an ExpectedActual struct into a readable string.\n// This function handles different value types and formats them side-by-side.\n// Returns empty string if the ExpectedActual is empty (both values nil).\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - ea: The ExpectedActual struct to format\n//\n// Returns a formatted string showing expected vs actual values, or empty string if nil/empty.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tea := NewExpectedActual(200, 404)\n//\tformatted := FormatExpectedActual(ea)\n//\t// Returns: \"expected: 200 (OK), actual: 404 (Not Found)\"\n//\n//\tea := NewExpectedActual(\"test@example.com\", \"invalid\")\n//\tformatted := FormatExpectedActual(ea)\n//\t// Returns: \"expected: 'test@example.com', actual: 'invalid'\"\nfunc FormatExpectedActual(ea ExpectedActual) string {\n\tif ea.IsEmpty() {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\tvar expectedStr, actualStr string\n\n\t// Format expected value\n\tif ea.HasExpected() {\n\t\tswitch exp := ea.Expected.(type) {\n\t\tcase int:\n\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", exp, getStatusCodeDescription(exp))\n\t\tcase []int:\n\t\t\tparts := make([]string, len(exp))\n\t\t\tfor i, code := range exp {\n\t\t\t\tparts[i] = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", code, getStatusCodeDescription(code))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"one of [%s]\", strings.Join(parts, \", \"))\n\t\tcase string:\n\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", exp)\n\t\tcase float64:\n\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%.2f\", exp)\n\t\tcase []string:\n\t\t\tquoted := make([]string, len(exp))\n\t\t\tfor i, s := range exp {\n\t\t\t\tquoted[i] = fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", s)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s]\", strings.Join(quoted, \", \"))\n\t\tcase map[string]interface{}:\n\t\t\texpectedStr = formatMapValue(exp)\n\t\tcase []interface{}:\n\t\t\texpectedStr = formatSliceValue(exp)\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", exp)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Format actual value\n\tif ea.HasActual() {\n\t\tswitch act := ea.Actual.(type) {\n\t\tcase int:\n\t\t\tactualStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", act, getStatusCodeDescription(act))\n\t\tcase string:\n\t\t\t// Truncate long strings\n\t\t\tif len(act) > 100 {\n\t\t\t\tact = act[:100] + \"...\"\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tactualStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", act)\n\t\tcase float64:\n\t\t\tactualStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%.2f\", act)\n\t\tcase []string:\n\t\t\tquoted := make([]string, len(act))\n\t\t\tfor i, s := range act {\n\t\t\t\tquoted[i] = fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", s)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tactualStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s]\", strings.Join(quoted, \", \"))\n\t\tcase map[string]interface{}:\n\t\t\tactualStr = formatMapValue(act)\n\t\tcase []interface{}:\n\t\t\tactualStr = formatSliceValue(act)\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\tactualStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", act)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Build the formatted string\n\tvar parts []string\n\tif expectedStr != \"\" {\n\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %s\", expectedStr))\n\t}\n\tif actualStr != \"\" {\n\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %s\", actualStr))\n\t}\n\n\tif len(parts) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\treturn strings.Join(parts, \", \")\n}\n\n// formatMapValue formats a map value for display in error messages.\nfunc formatMapValue(m map[string]interface{}) string {\n\tif len(m) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"{}\"\n\t}\n\n\tvar pairs []string\n\tfor k, v := range m {\n\t\tpairs = append(pairs, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %v\", k, v))\n\t}\n\n\t// Limit output for large maps\n\tif len(pairs) > 5 {\n\t\tpairs = pairs[:5]\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"{%s, ...}\", strings.Join(pairs, \", \"))\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"{%s}\", strings.Join(pairs, \", \"))\n}\n\n// formatSliceValue formats a slice value for display in error messages.\nfunc formatSliceValue(s []interface{}) string {\n\tif len(s) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"[]\"\n\t}\n\n\tvar items []string\n\tfor _, v := range s {\n\t\titems = append(items, fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", v))\n\t}\n\n\t// Limit output for large slices\n\tif len(items) > 5 {\n\t\titems = items[:5]\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s, ...]\", strings.Join(items, \", \"))\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s]\", strings.Join(items, \", \"))\n}\n\n// FormatExpectedActualInline formats ExpectedActual values inline (compact format).\n// This is useful for embedding in error messages where space is limited.\n// Returns empty string if the ExpectedActual is empty.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - ea: The ExpectedActual struct to format\n//\n// Returns a compact formatted string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tea := NewExpectedActual(200, 404)\n//\tformatted := FormatExpectedActualInline(ea)\n//\t// Returns: \"(expected 200, got 404)\"\nfunc FormatExpectedActualInline(ea ExpectedActual) string {\n\tif ea.IsEmpty() {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\tvar parts []string\n\tif ea.HasExpected() {\n\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"expected %v\", ea.Expected))\n\t}\n\tif ea.HasActual() {\n\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"got %v\", ea.Actual))\n\t}\n\n\tif len(parts) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"(%s)\", strings.Join(parts, \", \"))\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// COMPREHENSIVE ERROR FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatValidationErrorFull creates a comprehensive, formatted error message from a ValidationError.\n// This combines all relevant error information into a single, readable message.\n// Unlike FormatValidationError in format_helpers.go, this takes a ValidationError struct.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n//   - context: Optional ValidationErrorContext for additional location and field information\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := ValidationError{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, true, nil)\n//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required\"\n//\n//\tctx := NewValidationErrorContext(\"line 5\").WithRelatedFields([]string{\"email_confirmation\"})\n//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, true, ctx)\n//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required (location: line 5, related fields: email_confirmation)\"\nfunc FormatValidationErrorFull(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool, context *ValidationErrorContext) string {\n\tvar builder strings.Builder\n\n\t// Add severity if requested\n\tif includeSeverity {\n\t\t// Try to get severity from ErrorType enum first\n\t\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(err.ErrorType)\n\t\tvar severity ErrorSeverity\n\t\tif et.IsValid() && et != ErrTypeUnknown {\n\t\t\tseverity = GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(et)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// Fall back to string-based error type lookup\n\t\t\tseverity = GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(err.ErrorType)\n\t\t}\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add error type and field\n\terrorTypeEnum := ErrorTypeFromString(err.ErrorType)\n\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorMessage(errorTypeEnum, err.Message, err.FieldName))\n\n\t// Add expected/actual values if present\n\tif err.Expected != nil || err.Actual != nil {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n\t\tif err.Expected != nil {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %v\", err.Expected))\n\t\t}\n\t\tif err.Expected != nil && err.Actual != nil {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\", \")\n\t\t}\n\t\tif err.Actual != nil {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %v\", err.Actual))\n\t\t}\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Add location if present in the error itself\n\tif err.Location != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" [%s]\", err.Location))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add context information if provided and not empty\n\tif context != nil && !context.IsEmpty() {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n\t\tvar contextParts []string\n\n\t\tif context.HasLocation() {\n\t\t\tcontextParts = append(contextParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"location: %s\", context.Location))\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif context.HasRelatedFields() {\n\t\t\tfieldsList := FormatFieldList(context.RelatedFields)\n\t\t\tcontextParts = append(contextParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"related fields: %s\", fieldsList))\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(strings.Join(contextParts, \", \"))\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Add suggestions if present\n\tif len(err.Suggestions) > 0 {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\"\\nSuggestions:\")\n\t\tfor _, suggestion := range err.Suggestions {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"\\n  - %s\", suggestion))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn builder.String()\n}\n\n// FormatValidationErrorWithExpectedActual creates a comprehensive, formatted error message\n// with optional ExpectedActual parameter for value comparison. This extends FormatValidationErrorFull\n// to support structured expected vs actual value display.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n//   - context: Optional ValidationErrorContext for additional location and field information\n//   - expectedActual: Optional ExpectedActual for structured value comparison display\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string with optional expected/actual comparison.\n//\n// The ExpectedActual parameter takes precedence over err.Expected/err.Actual when provided.\n// If expectedActual is nil or empty, the function falls back to err.Expected/err.Actual.\n// If both are empty, no value comparison is included.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := ValidationError{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}\n//\n//\t// With ExpectedActual parameter\n//\tea := NewExpectedActual(200, 404)\n//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorWithExpectedActual(err, true, nil, ea)\n//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required (expected: 200 (OK), actual: 404 (Not Found))\"\n//\n//\t// Without ExpectedActual parameter (falls back to err.Expected/err.Actual)\n//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorWithExpectedActual(err, true, nil, ExpectedActual{})\n//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required\"\nfunc FormatValidationErrorWithExpectedActual(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool, context *ValidationErrorContext, expectedActual ExpectedActual) string {\n\tvar builder strings.Builder\n\n\t// Add severity if requested\n\tif includeSeverity {\n\t\t// Try to get severity from ErrorType enum first\n\t\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(err.ErrorType)\n\t\tvar severity ErrorSeverity\n\t\tif et.IsValid() && et != ErrTypeUnknown {\n\t\t\tseverity = GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(et)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// Fall back to string-based error type lookup\n\t\t\tseverity = GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(err.ErrorType)\n\t\t}\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add error type and field\n\terrorTypeEnum := ErrorTypeFromString(err.ErrorType)\n\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorMessage(errorTypeEnum, err.Message, err.FieldName))\n\n\t// Add expected/actual values - prefer ExpectedActual parameter if provided and non-empty\n\tif !expectedActual.IsEmpty() {\n\t\tformattedEA := FormatExpectedActual(expectedActual)\n\t\tif formattedEA != \"\" {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (%s)\", formattedEA))\n\t\t}\n\t} else if err.Expected != nil || err.Actual != nil {\n\t\t// Fall back to err.Expected/err.Actual\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n\t\tif err.Expected != nil {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %v\", err.Expected))\n\t\t}\n\t\tif err.Expected != nil && err.Actual != nil {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\", \")\n\t\t}\n\t\tif err.Actual != nil {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %v\", err.Actual))\n\t\t}\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Add location if present in the error itself\n\tif err.Location != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" [%s]\", err.Location))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add context information if provided and not empty\n\tif context != nil && !context.IsEmpty() {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n\t\tvar contextParts []string\n\n\t\tif context.HasLocation() {\n\t\t\tcontextParts = append(contextParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"location: %s\", context.Location))\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif context.HasRelatedFields() {\n\t\t\tfieldsList := FormatFieldList(context.RelatedFields)\n\t\t\tcontextParts = append(contextParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"related fields: %s\", fieldsList))\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(strings.Join(contextParts, \", \"))\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Add suggestions if present\n\tif len(err.Suggestions) > 0 {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\"\\nSuggestions:\")\n\t\tfor _, suggestion := range err.Suggestions {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"\\n  - %s\", suggestion))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn builder.String()\n}\n\n// FormatValidationErrorBrief creates a brief, one-line error message.\n// This is useful for summaries and compact displays.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n//\n// Returns a brief error message string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := ValidationError{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorBrief(err)\n//\t// Returns: \"email: required\"\nfunc FormatValidationErrorBrief(err ValidationError) string {\n\tif err.FieldName != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %s\", err.FieldName, err.ErrorType)\n\t}\n\n\treturn err.ErrorType\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR LIST FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatErrorList formats multiple errors into a readable list.\n// This is useful for displaying multiple validation failures.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to format\n//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n//\n// Returns a formatted error list string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}, {\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeFormat),\n//\t    Message: \"Invalid format\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n//\t}}\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorList(errors, true)\n//\t// Returns:\n//\t// 1. [High] [required] email: Field is required\n//\t// 2. [High] [format] password: Invalid format\nfunc FormatErrorList(errors []ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n\t}\n\n\tvar lines []string\n\tfor i, err := range errors {\n\t\tprefix := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d. \", i+1)\n\t\tformatted := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity, nil)\n\t\tlines = append(lines, prefix+formatted)\n\t}\n\n\treturn strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\")\n}\n\n// FormatErrorListWithContext formats multiple errors into a readable list with context information.\n// This is useful for displaying multiple validation failures with additional context for each error.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to format\n//   - contexts: Slice of ValidationErrorContext structs, one per error (can contain nil values)\n//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n//\n// Returns a formatted error list string.\n//\n// If contexts is nil or shorter than errors, missing contexts are treated as nil (no context).\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}, {\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeFormat),\n//\t    Message: \"Invalid format\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n//\t}}\n//\n//\tcontexts := []*ValidationErrorContext{\n//\t    NewValidationErrorContext(\"line 5\").WithRelatedFields([]string{\"email_confirmation\"}),\n//\t    NewValidationErrorContext(\"line 10\"),\n//\t}\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorListWithContext(errors, contexts, true)\n//\t// Returns:\n//\t// 1. [High] [required] email: Field is required (location: line 5, related fields: email_confirmation)\n//\t// 2. [High] [format] password: Invalid format (location: line 10)\nfunc FormatErrorListWithContext(errors []ValidationError, contexts []*ValidationErrorContext, includeSeverity bool) string {\n\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n\t}\n\n\tvar lines []string\n\tfor i, err := range errors {\n\t\tprefix := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d. \", i+1)\n\n\t\tvar context *ValidationErrorContext\n\t\tif contexts != nil && i < len(contexts) {\n\t\t\tcontext = contexts[i]\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tformatted := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity, context)\n\t\tlines = append(lines, prefix+formatted)\n\t}\n\n\treturn strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\")\n}\n\n// FormatErrorListSummary creates a summary of multiple errors.\n// This provides a high-level overview of validation failures.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to summarize\n//\n// Returns a summary string with error counts by type.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}, {\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n//\t}}\n//\n//\tsummary := FormatErrorListSummary(errors)\n//\t// Returns: \"2 errors: required (2)\"\nfunc FormatErrorListSummary(errors []ValidationError) string {\n\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Count errors by type\n\tcounts := make(map[string]int)\n\tfor _, err := range errors {\n\t\tcounts[err.ErrorType]++\n\t}\n\n\t// Build summary parts\n\tvar parts []string\n\tfor errorType, count := range counts {\n\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%d)\", errorType, count))\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%d error(s): %s\", len(errors), strings.Join(parts, \", \"))\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// CONVENIENCE WRAPPER FUNCTIONS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatFieldLocation is a convenience wrapper for FormatFieldLocationWith\n// without the parent parameter.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - location: Additional location context\n//\n// Returns a formatted location string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tloc := FormatFieldLocation(\"email\", \"line 5\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email at line 5\"\nfunc FormatFieldLocation(fieldName string, location string) string {\n\treturn FormatFieldLocationWith(fieldName, location, \"\")\n}\n\n// FormatFieldList is a convenience wrapper for FormatFieldListWith\n// with \"and\" as the default conjunction.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fields: Slice of field names\n//\n// Returns a formatted list string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlist := FormatFieldList([]string{\"email\", \"password\"})\n//\t// Returns: \"email and password\"\nfunc FormatFieldList(fields []string) string {\n\treturn FormatFieldListWith(fields, \"and\")\n}\n\n// FormatValidationErrorToString is an alias for FormatValidationErrorFull without context.\n// This provides a more explicit name for converting a ValidationError to a string.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string.\nfunc FormatValidationErrorToString(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n\treturn FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity, nil)\n}\n\n// FormatValidationErrorWithContext creates a comprehensive, formatted error message from a ValidationError\n// with optional context information. This is a convenience function that simplifies the common case\n// of formatting an error with context.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n//   - *testing.T) {\n402\t\t// Comprehensive enum handling tests\n403\t\tt.Run(\"all ErrorType enum values work correctly\", func(t *testing.T) {\n404\t\t\tallTypes := []ErrorType{\n405\t\t\t\tErrTypeRequired,\n406\t\t\t\tErrTypeFormat,\n407\t\t\t\tErrTypeRange,\n408\t\t\t\tErrTypeLength,\n409\t\t\t\tErrTypeType,\n410\t\t\t\tErrTypeValue,\n411\t\t\t\tErrTypeDuplicate,\n412\t\t\t\tErrTypeConflict,\n413\t\t\t\tErrTypeUnknown,\n414\t\t\t}\n415\t\n416\t\t\tfor _, et := range allTypes {\n417\t\t\t\tresult := FormatErrorMessage(et, \"Test message\", \"field\")\n418\t\n419\t\t\t\t// All valid types except ErrTypeUnknown should include error type in brackets\n420\t\t\t\tif et != ErrTypeUnknown {\n421\t\t\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(result, fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s]\", et.String())) {\n422\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"ErrorType %v should produce output with error type in brackets, got %v\", et, result)\n423\t\t\t\t\t}\n424\t\t\t\t} else {\n425\t\t\t\t\t// ErrTypeUnknown should not include error type in brackets\n426\t\t\t\t\tif strings.Contains(result, \"[unknown]\") {\n427\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"ErrTypeUnknown should not produce output with error type in brackets, got %v\", result)\n428\t\t\t\t\t}\n429\t\t\t\t}\n430\t\n431\t\t\t\t// Check that result contains field name\n432\t\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(result, \"field\") {\n433\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"ErrorType %v should produce output containing field name, got %v\", et, result)\n434\t\t\t\t}\n435\t\n436\t\t\t\t// Check that result contains message\n437\t\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(result, \"Test message\") {\n438\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"ErrorType %v should produce output containing message, got %v\", et, result)\n439\t\t\t\t}\n440\t\t\t}\n441\t\t})\n442\t\n443\t\tt.Run(\"enum and string wrapper produce consistent results\", func(t *testing.T) {\n444\t\t\tmessage := \"Field is required\"\n445\t\t\tfieldName := \"email\"\n446\t\n447\t\t\t// Test with ErrorType enum\n448\t\t\tenumResult := FormatErrorMessage(ErrTypeRequired, message, fieldName)\n449\t\n450\t\t\t// Test with string wrapper\n451\t\t\tstringResult := FormatErrorMessageString(\"required\", message, fieldName)\n452\t\n453\t\t\tif enumResult != stringResult {\n454\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Enum and string wrapper should produce same result:\\n  Enum:   %v\\n  String: %v\", enumResult, stringResult)\n455\t\t\t}\n456\t\t})\n457\t\n458\t\tt.Run(\"string wrapper converts all valid types correctly\", func(t *testing.T) {\n459\t\t\ttypeMappings := []struct {\n460\t\t\t\tstr      string\n461\t\t\t\tenum     ErrorType\n462\t\t\t\tmessage  string\n463\t\t\t\tfield    string\n464\t\t\t}{\n465\t\t\t\t{\"required\", ErrTypeRequired, \"Required\", \"field1\"},\n466\t\t\t\t{\"format\", ErrTypeFormat, \"Invalid format\", \"field2\"},\n467\t\t\t\t{\"range\", ErrTypeRange, \"Out of range\", \"field3\"},\n468\t\t\t\t{\"length\", ErrTypeLength, \"Too long\", \"field4\"},\n469\t\t\t\t{\"type\", ErrTypeType, \"Wrong type\", \"field5\"},\n470\t\t\t\t{\"value\", ErrTypeValue, \"Invalid value\", \"field6\"},\n471\t\t\t\t{\"duplicate\", ErrTypeDuplicate, \"Duplicate\", \"field7\"},\n472\t\t\t\t{\"conflict\", ErrTypeConflict, \"Conflict\", \"field8\"},\n473\t\t\t\t{\"unknown\", ErrTypeUnknown, \"Unknown error\", \"field9\"},\n474\t\t\t}\n475\t\n476\t\t\tfor _, tm := range typeMappings {\n477\t\t\t\tenumResult := FormatErrorMessage(tm.enum, tm.message, tm.field)\n478\t\t\t\tstringResult := FormatErrorMessageString(tm.str, tm.message, tm.field)\n479\t\n480\t\t\t\tif enumResult != stringResult {\n481\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Type mapping mismatch for %s:\\n  Enum:   %v\\n  String: %v\", tm.str, enumResult, stringResult)\n482\t\t\t\t}\n483\t\t\t}\n484\t\t})\n485\t\n486\t\tt.Run(\"ErrTypeUnknown special handling\", func(t *testing.T) {\n487\t\t\t// ErrTypeUnknown should not include error type in brackets\n488\t\t\tresult := FormatErrorMessage(ErrTypeUnknown, \"Test message\", \"field\")\n489\t\n490\t\t\tif strings.Contains(result, \"[unknown]\") {\n491\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"ErrTypeUnknown should not include error type in brackets, got: %v\", result)\n492\t\t\t}\n493\t\n494\t\t\t// But should still include field and message\n495\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(result, \"field\") {\n496\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Result should contain field name, got: %v\", result)\n497\t\t\t}\n498\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(result, \"Test message\") {\n499\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Result should contain message, got: %v\", result)\n500\t\t\t}\n501\t\n502\t\t\texpected := \"field: Test message\"\n503\t\t\tif result != expected {\n504\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"ErrTypeUnknown handling incorrect, got: %v, want: %v\", result, expected)\n505\t\t\t}\n506\t\t})\n507\t\n508\t\tt.Run(\"case-insensitive string conversion\", func(t *testing.T) {\n509\t\t\t// Test case-insensitive conversion in string wrapper\n510\t\t\tlowercaseResult := FormatErrorMessageString(\"required\", \"Test\", \"field\")\n511\t\t\tuppercaseResult := FormatErrorMessageString(\"REQUIRED\", \"Test\", \"field\")\n512\t\t\tmixedcaseResult := FormatErrorMessageString(\"ReQuIrEd\", \"Test\", \"field\")\n513\t\n514\t\t\t// All should produce the same result\n515\t\t\tif lowercaseResult != uppercaseResult {\n516\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Case-insensitive conversion failed: 'required' vs 'REQUIRED'\\n  %v\\n  %v\", lowercaseResult, uppercaseResult)\n517\t\t\t}\n518\t\t\tif lowercaseResult != mixedcaseResult {\n519\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Case-insensitive conversion failed: 'required' vs 'ReQuIrEd'\\n  %v\\n  %v\", lowercaseResult, mixedcaseResult)\n520\t\t\t}\n521\t\n522\t\t\texpected := \"[required] field: Test\"\n523\t\t\tif lowercaseResult != expected {\n524\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Case-insensitive result incorrect, got: %v, want: %v\", lowercaseResult, expected)\n525\t\t\t}\n526\t\t})\n527\t\n528\t\tt.Run(\"invalid string types convert to ErrTypeUnknown\", func(t *testing.T) {\n529\t\t\tinvalidTypes := []string{\"invalid_type\", \"custom_error\", \"random_type\", \"\"}\n530\t\n531\t\t\tfor _, invalidType := range invalidTypes {\n532\t\t\t\tresult := FormatErrorMessageString(invalidType, \"Test message\", \"field\")\n533\t\n534\t\t\t\t// Should not include error type in brackets for invalid types\n535\t\t\t\tif strings.Contains(result, \"[invalid_type]\") || strings.Contains(result, \"[custom_error]\") {\n536\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Invalid type '%s' should not appear in brackets, got: %v\", invalidType, result)\n537\t\t\t\t}\n538\t\n539\t\t\t\t// But should still include field and message\n540\t\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(result, \"field\") {\n541\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Result should contain field name for invalid type '%s', got: %v\", invalidType, result)\n542\t\t\t\t}\n543\t\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(result, \"Test message\") {\n544\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Result should contain message for invalid type '%s', got: %v\", invalidType, result)\n545\t\t\t\t}\n546\t\t\t}\n547\t\t})\n548\t\n549\t\tt.Run(\"empty field and message handling with enum\", func(t *testing.T) {\n550\t\t\t// Test empty field name\n551\t\t\tresult1 := FormatErrorMessage(ErrTypeRequired, \"Test message\", \"\")\n552\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(result1, \"[required]\") {\n553\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Empty field name should still include error type, got: %v\", result1)\n554\t\t\t}\n555\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(result1, \"Test message\") {\n556\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Empty field name should still include message, got: %v\", result1)\n557\t\t\t}\n558\t\n559\t\t\t// Test with only error type (empty message and field)\n560\t\t\tresult2 := FormatErrorMessage(ErrTypeFormat, \"\", \"\")\n561\t\t\t// Should just be empty since message is empty\n562\t\t\tif result2 != \"\" {\n563\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Empty message and field should produce empty result, got: %v\", result2)\n564\t\t\t}\n565\t\t})\n566\t}\n567\t\n568\tfunc TestFormatErrorWithValues(t *testing.T) {\n569\t\ttests := []struct {\n570\t\t\tname          string\n571\t\t\tfieldName     string\n572\t\t\texpected      interface{}\n573\t\t\tactual        interface{}\n574\t\t\tcustomMessage string\n575\t\t\texpectedMsg   string\n576\t\t}{\n577\t\t\t{\n578\t\t\t\tname:          \"with field name\",\n579\t\t\t\tfieldName:     \"status_code\",\n580\t\t\t\texpected:      200,\n581\t\t\t\tactual:    50,\n598\t\t\t\tcustomMessage: \"\",\n599\t\t\t\texpectedMsg:   \"expected 100, got 50\",\n600\t\t\t},\n601\t\t\t{\n602\t\t\t\tname:          \"string values\",\n603\t\t\t\tfieldName:     \"type\",\n604\t\t\t\texpected:      \"number\",\n605\t\t\t\tactual:        \"string\",\n606\t\t\t\tcustomMessage: \"\",\n607\t\t\t\texpectedMsg:   \"type: expected number, got tt.expectedMsg)\n616\t\t\t\t}\n617\t\t\t})\n618\t\t}\n619\t}\n620\t\n621\tfunc TestFormatErrorWithRange(t *testing.T) {\n622\t\ttests := []struct {\n623\t\t\tname        string\n624\t\t\tfieldName FormatFieldListWith(tt.fields, tt.conjunction)\n\t\t\tif result != tt.expected {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"FormatFieldListWith() = %v, want %v\", result, tt.expected)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING TESTS\n// =============================================================================\n\nfunc TestFormatErrorMessage(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname      string\n\t\terrorType ErrorType\n\t\tmessage   string\n\t\tfieldName string\n\t\texpected  string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"with all components\",\n\t\t\terrorType: ErrTypeRequired,\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Field is required\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"email\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"[required] email: Field is required\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"without field name\",\n\t\t\terrorType: ErrTypeFormat,\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Invalid email format\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"[format] Invalid email format\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"message only\",\n\t\t\terrorType: ErrTypeUnknown,\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Some error occurred\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"Some error occurred\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"whitespace handling\",\n\t\t\terrorType: ErrTypeRequired,\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"  Field is required  \",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"  email  \",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"[required] email: Field is required\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"range error type\",\n\t\t\terrorType: ErrTypeRange,\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Value out of range\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"age\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"[range] age: Value out of range\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"type error type\",\n\t\t\terrorType: ErrTypeType,\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Wrong type\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"count\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"[type] count: Wrong type\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"length error type\",\n\t\t\terrorType: ErrTypeLength,\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Too short\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"password\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"[length] password: Too short\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"value error type\",\n\t\t\terrorType: ErrTypeValue,\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Invalid value\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"status\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"[value] status: Invalid value\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"duplicate error type\",\n\t\t\terrorType: ErrTypeDuplicate,\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Already
